description: You are a museum curator developing a digital 'blink comparator' exhibit about the discovery of Pluto. Recreate the sky conditions Clyde Tombaugh observed at Lowell Observatory (35.198°N, 111.651°W, 2210m) by simulating pristine photographic plates. Configure the simulation to disable atmosphere, landscape, and deep-sky objects while enabling the equatorial coordinate grid. Capture two screenshots centered on the star 'Wasat' (Delta Geminorum) with a ~2-degree Field of View: Plate A at January 23, 1930, 06:00:00 UTC, and Plate B at January 29, 1930, 06:00:00 UTC, ensuring the camera remains centered on Wasat for both. Save the application settings and write an exhibit caption to /home/ga/Desktop/tombaugh_exhibit.txt. The caption must explicitly mention 'Jan 23', 'Jan 29', '1930', 'Pluto', and either 'Wasat' or 'Gemini'.
